title: validate_stmt and collect_regex_patterns duplicate clause-walk logic
description: lib.rs contains two functions — validate_stmt (line 333) and collect_regex_patterns (line 442) — that independently implement identical clause-boundary tracking: has_regex_tag, has_matches_tag, clause_start, the Block branch resetting state, and the TestList recursion. They even use the same helper (cache_patterns_in_clause / validate_regex_in_clause) to act on each clause. The duplication is a maintenance hazard: if a third match-type tag is added (e.g., a hypothetical :pcre extension), it must be handled in both walks. Missing one produces a script that either crashes silently at eval time (validate finds nothing wrong) or wastes cache memory (collect runs but validate rejects). Fix: extract the shared clause-walk into a single generic visitor that takes a per-clause callback, or combine the two passes into one. Either way, the logic lives in one place.
